reasoning: Scarlet fever is caused by Group A Streptococcus (Strep A). The Wikipedia article list of infectious diseases explicitly showed "No" for vaccines as of March 6, 2026. A search for recent developments up to mid-March 2026 reveals ongoing research (such as mRNA vaccines encoding conserved Strep A antigens and other preclinical and clinical candidates as recently as mid-to-late 2025). One paper from July 2025 explicitly states "There are currently no licensed vaccines to prevent Group A Streptococcus (GAS) infections." Given that developing, testing, approving, and distributing a vaccine takes a considerable amount of time (typically years for trials and approval), the probability that a vaccine would be completed, approved, announced, and then updated on Wikipedia between March 6 and March 15/22, 2026, without appearing in recent news or literature prior to March 15, is negligible. Therefore, I predict the lowest allowable probability of 0.02.
